untranslatable_reason: BARR pattern detection requires trend line identification and multi-bump formation analysis. The allowed indicators do not include trend line construction, slope measurement, or geometric pattern recognition needed to identify: (1) down-sloping trend line touch points, (2) bump formation sequences, (3) dual-bump occurrence detection (~20% occurrence rate), and (4) breakout confirmation above trend line. These are visual/structural pattern elements that require custom pattern detection logic beyond the available technical indicators.
translation_notes: Signal describes a complex chart pattern (Bump-and-Run Reversal Bottom) that fundamentally relies on trend line geometry and price action pattern recognition. While a simplified proxy using moving average crossovers has been provided as a fallback entry condition, this does not capture the true BARR pattern mechanics. Full implementation would require custom pattern detection functions in the backtest engine.
